name: Tasks MCP Server
description: The cw-tasks MCP stdio handler (tasks-server.mjs) that exposes the workspace task board to Claude sessions: task_list, task_create, task_update, task_delete, task_find_similar. All persistence goes through the shared task-store; this module owns the TOOL SURFACE, the per-session worktree SCOPING of task_list, and the text RENDERING of the board. Spawned behind the unified cw-code endpoint (code-server.mjs routes tools/call verbatim to handle()). Per-session identity comes from env: CODE_WORKBENCH_REPO_KEY (bucket), CODE_WORKBENCH_REPO_PATH (main repo), CODE_WORKBENCH_WORKTREE_PATH (the worktree the session runs in → WORKTREE_KEY); each falls back to walking up from cwd, so it works for any project the workbench opens.
guidelines: task_list default scope (no `worktree` arg — how cw-work calls it) returns tasks whose ROOT-ancestor worktree key === this session's WORKTREE_KEY, PLUS unassigned tasks (root worktree key === "") rendered as root-level `[unassigned]`. An explicit `worktree` arg is a deliberate narrowing: it returns exactly that worktree and EXCLUDES the unassigned backlog. `worktree: ""` (or a session with no WORKTREE_KEY) makes wantKey falsy, skips the `if (wantKey)` filter, and returns ALL tasks (the everything-view).

Scope subtasks by their ROOT ancestor's worktree, never their own — a subtask always has worktree null (enforced by task-store). Walk parentId over the UNFILTERED rawTasks (with a `seen` cycle guard) so status/worktree filtering can't break the lineage walk.

The renderer only descends from root tasks, so after filtering, prune any subtask whose parent didn't survive (or is an orphan) to a fixed point BEFORE computing total/limit — otherwise unrenderable tasks inflate `total` and the '(N more)' truncation footer. The prune is render-invariant: it only removes tasks that could never appear.

Only ROOT tasks carry the `[unassigned]` marker (depth===0 with falsy worktree); subtasks inherit the root's assignment and stay unmarked. The cw-work skill must skip at root granularity (skip an `[unassigned]` root AND its subtasks), not by the per-line marker.

The cw-work skill is the contract partner: it may SEE unassigned tasks but must not auto-start them unless the user explicitly asks; claiming one means task_update worktree=<this worktree's root folder basename> (the server lowercases it). Keep the tool descriptions and the skill text in sync when scoping changes.
anti_patterns: Do not filter subtasks by their own `worktree` field — it is always null; use the root-ancestor worktree via the parentId walk.

Do not count tasks toward total/limit that the renderer can't show (subtasks of a filtered-out or deleted parent) — prune them first, or the truncation footer lies and a real task can be silently dropped past the limit.
Do not make an explicit `worktree` arg include the unassigned backlog — that path is an exact narrowing; only the default scope mixes in unassigned.

Do not run `prettier --write` on this file (or any packages/mcp-core/*.mjs/.cjs) — the package is committed in a double-quote / ~80-col style that does NOT match the repo .prettierrc.json (singleQuote, 100-col); reformatting produces a huge spurious diff. Match the surrounding style by hand.
Do not add task persistence logic here — all reads/writes go through @code-workbench/mcp-core/task-store.
decisions: task_list surfaces the unassigned backlog in the default worktree scope (marked `[unassigned]`) so cw-work and the user can see shared, unclaimed work from any worktree session — mirroring the desktop app / extension task panels, which already render their own 'Unassigned' group from raw task.worktree. The cw-work skill enforces 'visible but not auto-started'.
Worktree scoping uses the stable worktreeKey (lowercased basename) from task-format so the same logical worktree matches across machines after a cross-machine git sync.
Kept as a separate component from task-store: task-store owns .md file I/O + CRUD; this server owns the MCP tool surface, worktree scoping, and board rendering.
